text:
Once upon a time in the quiet town of Willow Creek, nestled between whispering woods and the gentle babbling brook, a peculiar old bell hung in the town hall. It was an ancient bell, adorned with tiny roses and faded enamels, said to have been cast by the fairies of fairyland. Every year on the Feast of Souls, when the moon was a silver spoon and the air was electric with mystery, the bell tolled three times. People came from far and wide to hear its haunting melody and remember their loved ones.

One October dusk, young Elara arrived early. She skipped through the hedge-clusters, her eyes glistening with excitement. Today was different; her mother had whispered tales of the midnight bells. She set off to find the bell tower, her little footsteps echoing. As she climbed through aged stone, the moonlight spun into golden threads.

At the top, she found it! The bell didn't ring. Instead, it sung a song of forgotten
